summary: Breaking change documentation: new SKFont() now eagerly sets SKTypeface.Empty (via upstream Skia m132) instead of lazily resolving to the platform default at draw time, causing text measurement and drawing to produce zero results for code that relied on the old null-typeface behavior.

codeSnippets: var font = new SKFont(); var width = font.MeasureText("Hello"); // returns 0 if typeface not set
font.Typeface = null; var tf = font.Typeface; // now returns SKTypeface.Empty, not null

repoLinks.description: Parent tracker: document and address behavioral breaking changes from Skia m147
Related: SKFont.GetTextPath returning empty paths for emojis — compounded by empty-typeface default

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Upstream Skia m132 removed the getTypefaceOrDefault() lazy resolution pattern. The C# parameterless constructor has been updated to pass SKTypeface.Default, but the null-setter path (font.Typeface = null) still passes IntPtr.Zero and will return SKTypeface.Empty from the getter.

analysis.summary: Upstream Skia m132 eliminated the lazy-typeface-resolution pattern: SKFont no longer stores null and resolves to platform default at draw time; instead it stores SkTypeface::MakeEmpty() when given null. The C# parameterless constructor (SKFont()) has already been updated to pass SKTypeface.Default, preserving the expected behavior. The remaining gap is: (a) tests for the BC1_* scenarios listed in the issue body are not yet written, and (b) users who set font.Typeface = null will silently get an empty typeface instead of null.
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/documentation because the issue was filed by the maintainer as a tracked breaking-change sub-issue of #3734, with explicit before/after behavior, migration guidance, and test IDs already defined. The C# fix (passing SKTypeface.Default in the parameterless constructor) is already present in binding/SkiaSharp/SKFont.cs. The remaining work is writing BC1_* test coverage and finalizing the migration docs.

codeInvestigation.finding: Parameterless SKFont() constructor now calls this(SKTypeface.Default, ...) — the C# migration fix is already applied. The Typeface setter still passes IntPtr.Zero for null (line 77), which in m132 causes sk_font_get_typeface to return SKTypeface.Empty.

SKTypeface.Empty is backed by sk_typeface_create_empty(). SKTypeface.Default uses sk_fontmgr_legacy_create_typeface to get the platform default, falling back to Empty if not found. IsEmpty checks GlyphCount == 0.

keySignals.text: new SKFont().Typeface → null (lazy resolve to platform default at draw time) → SKTypeface.Empty (empty typeface, 0 glyphs)
keySignals.interpretation: Behavioral breaking change: code relying on null-typeface lazy resolution now gets empty typeface and zero-width text.
The null-setter path still passes IntPtr.Zero; the getter now returns Empty instead of null.
Four test cases planned but not yet present in the test suite — confirmed by code search.

keySignals.text: Possibly related: #3484 (open PR) — SKFont.GetTextPath returning empty paths for emojis.
keySignals.interpretation: The v4 empty-typeface default compounds the GetTextPath issue; migration guidance should reference #3484.

nextQuestions: Are the BC1_* tests blocked on anything or ready to be written against current bindings?
Should the migration guidance be added to the official 4.x upgrade guide / changelog in this repo?
workarounds: Use new SKFont(SKTypeface.Default) instead of new SKFont() to get a font that can measure and draw text.
Check typeface.IsEmpty instead of typeface == null to detect 'no typeface set'.

resolution.hypothesis: The upstream Skia change is intentional and the C# parameterless constructor fix is correct. Remaining work: write the four BC1_* unit tests, and optionally update the 4.x migration guide with this entry.

proposals.title: Write BC1_* unit tests
proposals.description: Add the four tests listed in the issue body to SKFontTest.cs: BC1_NewSKFontTypefaceIsEmpty, BC1_NewSKFontMeasureTextReturnsZero, BC1_SKFontSetTypefaceNullReturnsEmpty, BC1_SKFontWithDefaultCanMeasure.

proposals.title: Update migration docs / changelog
proposals.description: Add a section in the 4.x upgrade guide documenting this breaking change, the migration path (use SKTypeface.Default), and how to check IsEmpty.

resolution.recommendedReason: Tests close the gap on the coverage listed in the issue and confirm the migration path. Docs update can follow.

actionability.reason: The C# fix is already applied (SKFont() uses SKTypeface.Default). The remaining work is writing four planned unit tests (BC1_*) and optionally updating the migration guide. No investigation needed — the root cause and fix are both documented in the issue body.

actions.description: Confirm triage and summarize remaining work
